hook.text: Auto-increment integers or UUIDs? The choice of primary key strategy affects performance and scalability.

buildup.text: Auto-increment IDs are simple and compact. But they reveal record count and ordering to anyone who sees them.

discovery.text: UUIDs are globally unique and hide ordering. But they're 16 bytes vs 4 bytes and fragment indexes.

twist.text: ULIDs and UUIDv7 are time-sorted UUIDs. Globally unique, yet index-friendly. The best of both worlds.

climax.text: Pick the strategy that fits: integers for simple apps, UUIDs for distributed systems.

punchline.text: IDs shape performance. Choose the right kind for your needs.

quiz.question: Why do UUIDs fragment database indexes?
options: Their random ordering causes inserts to scatter across index pages instead of appending
UUIDs are too large to fit in an index
